reasoning: The informal statement specifies an "integral domain," which by standard mathematical convention (and per Dummit & Foote) is a commutative ring with no zero divisors. The corrected formalization uses `[CommRing R] [IsDomain R]`, matching this convention. The original uses `[Ring R] [IsDomain R]`; in Mathlib, `IsDomain` is defined over any `Semiring` and does not imply commutativity — the docstring at `Mathlib/Algebra/Ring/Defs.lean:425` explicitly instructs: "To obtain an integral domain use `[CommRing α] [IsDomain α]`." Thus the Original drops the commutativity premise inherent in "integral domain," yielding a more general (stronger) statement. The statement is still provable: `sq_eq_one_iff` in `Mathlib/Algebra/Ring/Commute.lean:176` holds under `[Ring R] [NoZeroDivisors R]`, and `IsDomain` implies `NoZeroDivisors` via `IsCancelMulZero`. So the Original is stronger than the informal claim but still true. The error is dropping the (explicit, by terminology) commutativity requirement.
